fields.description:
## Why

The plan-review attestation's freshness is currently bound to the exact code HEAD sha
(`attest.py` `claim_gate_check` step 3: `result.head_sha != head` → `stale-head`). This is
too coarse in BOTH directions:

- **Over-invalidation (child bug `worm-folly-barge`):** ANY commit — even an unrelated file or a
  docs typo — invalidates a freshly-earned, still-correct attestation, forcing a slow (~3-5 min),
  billable re-review for no semantic reason. Especially painful on multi-ticket branches and
  shared-checkout test runs that move HEAD.
- **Under-invalidation (this epic's core goal):** the binding is to the WHOLE commit, not to the
  code the review was actually grounded against, so the design also fails to model "the cited code
  drifted" precisely.

Fix: scope attestation freshness to **what the review depended on, not who/what changed the rest of
the tree** — the content of the dependency files — using a content-addressed (Merkle) binding. This
matches the consensus of every sound incremental-staleness system (Gerrit `copyCondition`
`has:unchanged-files`, Bazel input-hashing, proof-caching dependency checksums). No per-commit
attribution, so the "sibling exemption" concern dissolves: a sibling's edit to a shared file is
treated exactly like any other edit.

## What

- **Dependency set** = union of the ticket's declared `file_impact` and the files cited in its
  `REVIEW_RESULT` findings (whole-file granularity).
- **Content-addressed binding:** at sign time, compute a `{path: content-hash}` map of the
  dependency set and store it **inside the signed manifest** (`build_manifest` / `sign_plan_review`)
  so drift detection is tamper-evident. (Today `head_sha` is recorded as audit metadata but is NOT
  in the signed payload — this also closes that latent integrity gap.)
- **Drift check at claim:** replace the whole-HEAD exact-match (`claim_gate_check` step 3) with a
  per-file content-hash comparison. Any mismatch / deletion / unexpected-creation of a dependency
  file = drift → block the claim (invalidate), prompting re-review. Content-addressing makes this
  ancestry-independent (immune to rebases and shared-checkout HEAD moves — the specific
  `worm-folly-barge` complaint).
- **Empty-set conservative fallback:** when the dependency set is empty (no `file_impact` AND no
  citations — common; this very epic has `file_impact: []`), we cannot scope, so fall back to
  invalidate-on-any-commit (today's safe behavior, but now ONLY for unscopable tickets).
- **New named DET-floor check (`P9` file-impact coverage):** `det_floor.py` currently has P1–P8; P4
  checks only the file-impact UPPER bound (`fi > cap`), not empty. Add a new, explicitly named check
  that, for **leaf tasks only** (`applies_at`), emits an **advisory finding (never blocks)** when
  `file_impact` is empty, advising the caller to specify the `{path, reason}` files this work will
  touch — so the attestation can be scoped to them (otherwise any commit invalidates it) and
  `next_batch` can schedule it conflict-free. Register it in `CANONICAL_DET`, the completeness guard,
  and the module docstring.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` — `build_manifest` / `sign_plan_review` (embed the signed
  `{path: content-hash}` map); `claim_gate_check` (replace step-3 whole-HEAD match with the per-file
  drift check; `head_sha` / `signed_at` remain audit metadata only).
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py` — new `p9_file_impact_coverage` advisory check + register
  in `CANONICAL_DET` + completeness guard + docstring.
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` — resolve the dependency set (`file_impact` via
  `get_file_impact` ∪ citations from the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar); wire `P9` at leaf `applies_at`.
- Tests under the plan-review test suite.

Out of scope (Story 2): the narrowed delta-re-review on drift. This story always falls back to a
FULL re-review when drift invalidates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The attestation binds a SIGNED `{path: content-hash}` map of the dependency set
      (`file_impact` ∪ review-cited files); the whole-HEAD exact-match staleness check is removed.
- [ ] Claim SUCCEEDS when an unrelated file changed after signing (fixes `worm-folly-barge`); claim
      is BLOCKED when a dependency file's content changed or was deleted (closes under-invalidation).
- [ ] Empty dependency set → conservative invalidate-on-any-commit fallback.
- [ ] New explicitly named DET check (`P9`) emits an advisory (non-blocking) warning on empty
      `file_impact` at leaf tasks, with guidance text; registered in `CANONICAL_DET` + completeness
      guard + module docstring. (Confirmed P4 does not already cover empty.)
- [ ] The drift check is content-addressed (rebase / HEAD-move immune) and cheap (sub-ms for a
      handful of files; no claim hot-path regression).
- [ ] The signed manifest serializes the dependency binding as a **per-path `{path: content-hash}`
      map** (NOT a single rolled-up root hash), so Story 2 (`grace-thud-feint`) can attach a
      per-finding code-hash vintage + reuse map on top of it. This payload shape is the contract
      fixed in ADR `0002-code-drift-invalidation.md`.
- [ ] **Claim-path check reads the SIGNED map, not a re-derived set.** At claim time the drift check
      iterates the `{path: hash}` entries IN the signed manifest and re-hashes exactly those paths —
      it does NOT recompute `file_impact ∪ citations` from current ticket state. (Re-deriving would
      let a post-sign `file_impact` shrink silently drop paths from the check and evade drift
      detection; the signed map is the authoritative, tamper-evident set.)
- [ ] **Citation extraction is schema-correct.** The "cited files" half of the dependency set is
      taken from the structured citation `path` (kind=`file`) on `REVIEW_RESULT` findings; if a
      finding exposes only a free-text `location` with no resolvable path, it is ignored (fall back
      to `file_impact`) rather than guessed. Verify the actual `REVIEW_RESULT`/findings schema and
      use the structured field; if none exists, that gap is part of this story's work.
- [ ] Tested: review at X, commit an unrelated file → claim succeeds; commit a dependency file →
      claim blocked; delete a dependency file → blocked; empty-set ticket → any commit blocks; `P9`
      fires on empty `file_impact` and is silent when populated.
